title: Parametrized hierarchical blocks are broken
body:
This line, added in a730daabef6f57fc13884f2dc3934568f50142ae disables prametrized hierarchical block lookup in the final re-integration run:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/60b52a4986281fff3e809a6df8764a7154dce90e/src/V3Param.cpp#L102-L103

It gates the first term in this condition:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/60b52a4986281fff3e809a6df8764a7154dce90e/src/V3Param.cpp#L1336

The effect is that in the final run, the stubs added for parametrized hierarchical blocks are ignored, and all the parametrized hierarchical blocks referenced from the root are re-compiled in the final run.

To demonstrate, add a print of `modelp->hierName()` in `VerilatedContext::addModel`, then run `t_hier_block.py`, it will show that the `delay` modules are not added as sub-models.

Alternatively, add `$display("foobar")"` inside `module delay` in the same test, then grep the generated sources for `foobar`. You will find it in both the compiled `Vdelay*` sub models, and in the root model. (They should only appear in the sub models)
